firstrun.no_presets: No presets found. Let's set up.
firstrun.pick_preset: Pick a preset for your orchestrator:
firstrun.select_hint: ↑↓ navigate  [enter] edit (on row) / activate button  [space] edit
firstrun.preset_cfg.title: Configure agent presets
firstrun.preset_cfg.help: Pick the default preset (the agent boots on this) and toggle which others it may swap to at runtime.
firstrun.preset_cfg.hint: ↑↓ navigate  [tab] jump to Next  [←→] switch buttons  [enter] set default / activate  [space] toggle  [ctrl+a] toggle all  [esc] back
firstrun.preset_cfg.cannot_unallow_defau: default preset must remain in the allowed list
firstrun.preset_cfg.empty: No saved presets yet — go back to the library and edit a template to create one.
firstrun.preset_cfg.no_saved_yet: No saved presets — edit a template first to create one.
firstrun.preset_key.hint: [tab] cycle textarea/buttons  [↑↓] move  [enter] activate button  [ctrl+e] external editor  [esc] back
firstrun.stamina_hint: awake time before auto-sleep (applies to all agents)
firstrun.context_limit_hint: max context window (applies to all agents)
firstrun.soul_delay_hint: seconds idle before agent thinks autonomously (default: 99999 ≈ silent). Soul flow is the agent's subconscious — it reflects on recent work, consults past selves, and surfaces insights. Set low (60-300) for proactive agents, mid (7200) for batch/workhorse agents, leave at default to silence it (applies to all agents)
firstrun.molt_pressure_hint: context % to trigger molt (applies to all agents)
firstrun.max_rpm_hint: API requests/min cap, shared across all agents on the same provider; 0 = no cap
firstrun.caps_recommend: Highly recommended to select all, especially on first run.
firstrun.caps_change_later: Use /setup and /refresh in chat to change later.
firstrun.addons_section: Add-ons — ask the agent to help you set up after creation
firstrun.cap_desc.file:
Read, write, edit, glob, and grep files.
The agent's hands on your filesystem. Scoped to its working directory by default.
firstrun.cap_desc.email:
Full email client — send, receive, reply, search, archive.
Needs an IMAP/SMTP account via the imap add-on, or a Gmail OAuth credential.
firstrun.cap_desc.bash:
Run shell commands with guardrails.
Lets the agent install tools, run scripts, and probe the system. Destructive commands require confirmation.
firstrun.cap_desc.web_search:
Search the web for up-to-date information.
Backend is chosen per provider — see the provider list on the right.
firstrun.cap_desc.psyche:
Evolving identity, pad, and context management.
The agent updates its own character and compresses context via molt. Highly recommended.
firstrun.cap_desc.vision:
Understand images.
The agent can read screenshots, photos, diagrams, and scanned documents.
firstrun.cap_desc.talk:
Text-to-speech.
Lets the agent generate audio replies. Requires a TTS-capable provider (see list on the right).
firstrun.cap_desc.draw:
Generate images from text prompts.
Useful for visualizations, drafts, and storyboards. Requires an image-capable provider.
firstrun.cap_desc.compose:
Generate music from text prompts.
Produces short instrumental or vocal clips. Requires a music-capable provider.
firstrun.cap_desc.video:
Generate short videos from text or image prompts.
Jobs are async and can take minutes. Requires a video-capable provider.
firstrun.cap_desc.listen:
Analyze audio — transcribe speech and appreciate music.
Useful for voice notes, meetings, and audio files. Defaults to a local whisper model.
firstrun.cap_desc.web_read:
Fetch and extract the content of web pages.
Gives the agent the ability to actually read articles found via web_search.
firstrun.cap_desc.avatar:
Spawn independent sub-agents (avatars).
Each avatar is a full agent in its own directory, running as a separate process. Ideal for long-running subtasks.
firstrun.cap_desc.daemon:
Spawn short-lived parallel workers.
Lighter than avatars — for fan-out work like "summarize these ten files in parallel".
firstrun.cap_desc.library:
Durable knowledge library.
Submit, filter, consolidate, and export notes. Survives molts — the agent's cumulative knowledge.
firstrun.cap_desc.skills:
Browse and use the skill catalog.
Skills are markdown playbooks the agent can load on demand, similar to Claude Code skills.
firstrun.cap_desc.soul:
Inner voice — periodic self-reflection.
The agent consults its past selves for advice when idle. Adjust cadence via /setup.
firstrun.cap_desc.system:
Lifecycle control — sleep, wake, notifications.
Manages the agent's process state and system-level operations.
firstrun.addon_desc.imap:
Connect any IMAP/SMTP mailbox.
Enables the email capability against Gmail, Outlook, Fastmail, etc. You'll provide host, username, and an app password.
firstrun.addon_desc.telegram:
Talk to the agent from Telegram.
Create a bot via @BotFather and paste its token. Optionally restrict to specific user IDs.
firstrun.addon_desc.feishu:
Talk to the agent from Feishu (Lark).
Create an enterprise self-built app at open.feishu.cn/app and paste its credentials.
firstrun.addon_desc.wechat:
Talk to the agent from WeChat.
Scan a QR code with your phone to connect — no API keys needed. Supports text, images, voice, video, and files.

settings.title: Settings
insight.auto_question: You are producing insights for the human operator. Based on everything in this conversation — the task, the context, domain knowledge, risks, and opportunities — produce exactly 2-3 bullet points of things the human would benefit from knowing. Each bullet should be a concrete, specific insight: a pattern you've noticed, relevant background knowledge, a risk they may not see, or an opportunity to explore. Format: start each bullet with '- ' on its own line. No preamble, no summary — just the bullets.
settings.language_desc: TUI display language. Changes take effect immediately.
settings.mail_page_size_desc: Max messages shown in the mail view. Larger = more scroll history.
settings.theme_desc: Color theme for the TUI interface.
settings.insights_desc: Auto-generate insights after each task. Use /insights for one-time.
settings.agent_lang_desc: Language the agent speaks. Requires /refresh to take effect.
settings.nickname_desc: Agents will address you by this name.
settings.agent_name_desc: Display name for the agent. Requires /refresh to take effect.
settings.nickname_hint: agents will address you by this name

doctor.title: Doctor
doctor.suggest_base_url: → Open preset editor and select a region (CN / INTL), then /refresh.
doctor.suggest_revive: → Provider is back. Try /refresh to restart.
doctor.suggest_venv: → Restart lingtai-tui so it can create its managed venv at ~/.lingtai-tui/runtime/venv.
doctor.migration_ahead: ✗ Project data version (v%d) is newer than this TUI (v%d) — you downgraded.
doctor.suggest_upgrade_tui: → Upgrade the TUI: brew upgrade lingtai-tui
doctor.migration_behind: ✗ Project is at v%d but TUI expects v%d — migrations did not complete.
doctor.suggest_restart_tui: → Quit and relaunch lingtai-tui at the project root to re-run migrations.
doctor.heartbeat_stale: ⚠ Orchestrator heartbeat: stale (%s ago) — asleep, suspended, or crashed. Check the main view for state.
doctor.heartbeat_missing: ⚠ Orchestrator heartbeat: not found — agent has not run since this project was created.
nirvana.title: Nirvana
nirvana.warning: ⚠ This will permanently destroy all data in the working directory.
nirvana.detail: All agents, mail history, configuration, and files will be permanently removed. You will return to the initial setup.

Built-in presets get refreshed by TUI upgrades.
Semantic edits (LLM, capabilities) are saved as a NEW preset
so the original stays pristine.

palette.export: Export a network or recipe (/export network, /export recipe)
export.no_agent: [system] No orchestrator running — start an agent first.
export.network_sent: [system] Asked the orchestrator to start the network export flow.
export.recipe_sent: [system] Asked the orchestrator to start the recipe export flow.
export.network_prompt: The human wants to export this network for sharing. Use the lingtai-recipe skill (read its SKILL.md first, then assets/export-network.md for the full network-export procedure) to walk them through the process. Start by greeting the human via email and explaining what you're about to do.
export.recipe_prompt: The human wants to export a recipe for sharing. Use the lingtai-recipe skill (read its SKILL.md first, then assets/export-recipe.md for the full recipe-export procedure) to walk them through the process. Start by greeting the human via email and explaining what you're about to do.
recipe.keep_current_desc: Save settings only — do not change the recipe or rewrite .prompt
recipe.title: Choose a launch recipe
recipe.hint: Recipes shape what your orchestrator says and how it behaves on launch.
recipe.desc.custom: Use your own greet.md and comment.md from a folder.
recipe.custom_path: Folder path (must contain greet.md and/or comment.md):
recipe.swap_hint: Changing from '%s' to '%s'. How should this apply?
recipe.swap_inplace_desc: Update the comment file and queue a new greet. Existing network preserved.
recipe.swap_fresh_desc: Destroy the current network and start over with the new recipe. Irreversible.
recipe.swap_nirvana_hint: To start fresh with a new recipe and wipe current history, use /nirvana.
cmd.btw: Ask the agent a side question (delivered as an insight inquiry — the agent reflects and responds without interrupting its current work)
cmd.sleep: Put agent to sleep (/sleep all for all agents). Sleeping agents preserve state and can be woken with /cpr
cmd.suspend: Suspend agent — freeze the process entirely (/suspend all for all agents). Must be revived with /cpr to resume
cmd.cpr: Revive a suspended or dead agent (/cpr all for all). Prefer /refresh for most cases — cpr is for agents that are down
cmd.clear: Clear the agent's entire context window and restart with a blank conversation. Identity, pad, and codex are preserved
cmd.refresh: Hard restart agent — reload init.json, capabilities, and all configuration from disk (/refresh all for all agents)
cmd.doctor: Diagnose connection issues — checks API keys, model availability, and network configuration
cmd.viz: Open the agent network visualization in browser (topology, mail flows, agent states). Requires lingtai-portal on PATH
cmd.addon: View and configure addon bridges (IMAP email, Telegram, Feishu, WeChat) that connect the agent to external messaging services
cmd.setup: Agent setup wizard — change LLM provider, model, capabilities, and runtime settings. Changes propagate to all agents
cmd.settings: TUI preferences — theme, mail page size, language, auto-insights toggle
cmd.kanban: Agent network dashboard — view all agents' properties, LLM config, capabilities, and context usage at a glance
cmd.projects: Browse registered projects and their agent networks
cmd.agora: Browse exported networks and recipes from the agora marketplace (/export network or /export recipe to share your own)
cmd.knowledge: Browse an agent's private knowledge — local decisions, notes, references, and migrated codex entries. Ctrl+T switches between agents
cmd.library: Browse an agent's durable knowledge library — accumulated research and findings. Ctrl+T switches between agents
cmd.insights: Request an insight from the agent now — the agent observes the current task and produces 2-3 concrete observations
cmd.skills: Browse the skill catalog — reusable procedures the agent can invoke on demand
cmd.system: Browse an agent's system files (system.md, covenant, principle, procedures, pad, llm). Ctrl+T switches between agents
cmd.mailbox: Browse all mailbox messages (inbox, sent, IMAP) with full message body and inline attachment rendering
cmd.presets: Open the preset library: view available presets, see their LLM and capabilities, and tag them with a 1–5 star cost/quality tier (higher is better). Tags propagate to agents via system(action='presets'); used for daemon/avatar selection. Read-only inspection plus tag editing — full preset creation still happens in /setup.
cmd.molt: Force the agent to molt immediately — saves context and resets the conversation window
cmd.nirvana: Wipe everything and start completely fresh — deletes .lingtai/ and all agent data. Irreversible
cmd.export: Export a network or recipe for sharing (/export network exports the full agent network, /export recipe exports a reusable recipe)
cmd.login: Show auth status for all saved credentials and re-authenticate if needed
cmd.quit: Quit lingtai-tui (agents continue running in background unless suspended first)
login.title: Login Status
login.no_credentials: No saved credentials. Use /setup to configure a provider.
